title: BURP Manoeuvre
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- For laryngoscopy intubation, where direct visualisation of the vocal cords is not possible.

#### Procedure

- Locate the thyroid cartilage.

- Assistant to place thumb and index fingers of one hand on thyroid cartilage.

- Firmly push the thyroid cartilage backwards, upwards (towards the patient’s head), and to the right (the patient’s right) (see **Figure 1**).

Figure 1

- Intubating paramedic guides assistant's hand until best view of the glottis is achieved.

- Maintain pressure until the intubating paramedic directs release of pressure post ET tube insertion.

#### Contraindications

- Nil when indicated.

#### Complications

- It is possible to obscure the glottis opening in small, or paediatric patients.
